what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600 by 900 visual composition showing seven tool icons feeding through dashed transport lines into a plug, a central hub, and six role nodes, with title text, blueprint corner underlays, a small V mark, and a collapsed footer band.
observations: Uses inline CSS and inline SVG inside a single HTML file.
Loads Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
The visual hierarchy is title, icon strip, plug terminal, hub, routed dashed paths, and role nodes.
Seven bronze line icons sit along a dashed rail at the top of the diagram.
Dashed bronze paths converge into a plug, then a gold lead descends into the hub.
Six role nodes are positioned evenly along the lower diagram area; five use gold rings and the last uses a bronze treatment.
A footer band with count copy exists in the markup but is hidden by a v2.1 correction override that turns it into a thin patterned foot.
Blueprint-style corner grid underlays are masked and low-opacity.
The marker definition uses a gold arrowhead shape on bronze dashed transport paths.
standouts: The composition expresses routing as a literal tree from tools to hub to roles.
The plug terminal creates a distinct intermediate junction between input tools and the hub.
The v2.1 correction leaves prior footer content in place while suppressing its visible copy.
conventions: Keeps all file-specific styling inline in the document head.
Uses CSS variables for palette, spacing, and typography tokens.
Builds the diagram as a single SVG with grouped transforms and explicit coordinates.
Uses comments as section labels for visual layers and revision notes.
Uses fixed canvas dimensions rather than responsive layout sizing.
Overrides earlier footer styles with later !important rules.
concerns: The file depends on external font and CSS variable resources to render as intended.
The 1600 by 900 .view is fixed-size, so embedding contexts need to handle scaling externally.
The hidden footer copy remains in the DOM despite being visually suppressed.
Several v2.1 footer correction rules use !important, which makes later local adjustment harder.
notes: This file is a self-contained visual diagram of tools routed through one hub to role-specific recipients.
questions: Should the hidden footer count text remain accessible or be removed from the markup?
Is the final bronze Role F treatment intended to mark a sequence end-step, a different state, or reduced emphasis?
Should the fixed 1600 by 900 frame be preserved for this artifact's target surface?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated routing-tree purpose matches the rendered content: multiple tools converge through one hub and branch to multiple roles.
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate that many tool inputs can be collected through one hub and distributed to different roles in a controlled workflow view.
novelty: The file combines a plug metaphor, hub-and-spoke routing, dashed transport rails, and visibly retained but suppressed footer markup in one diagram artifact.
contribution: Contributes a visual routing topology: tool inputs, convergence terminal, central hub, branch paths, role endpoints, and understated blueprint/footing treatments that a fused system could keep as a substrate for role-based workflow diagrams.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed-size HTML/SVG composition depicting seven tool icons routed through a plug and central hub to six role nodes, styled with bronze, gold, blueprint grids, and a collapsed footer band.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a role-based workflow routing concept where many tools feed one hub that distributes the right view to each role.
